  "text": "The Grafana permission model is built on a comprehensive three-tier role hierarchy (Viewer/Editor/Admin) defined across multiple packages:\n\n**Core Role Definitions:**\nThe three-tier role types are defined in `pkg/services/org/model.go` as `RoleViewer`, `RoleEditor`, and `RoleAdmin`. These are the base organization-level roles.\n\n**Role-to-Permission Mapping:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go` declares fixed roles and registers their assignments to the three built-in roles through the `DeclareFixedRoles()` function. The `BuildBasicRoleDefinitions()` function creates the basic role definitions with empty permissions initially. \n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go` explicitly maps dashboard permissions to the three tiers:\n- `DashboardViewActions` (read-only): assigned to \"Viewer\" role\n- `DashboardEditActions` (read+write+delete): assigned to \"Editor\" role  \n- `DashboardAdminActions` (read+write+delete+permissions): assigned to \"Admin\" role\nThe `registerDashboardRoles()` function registers these role-to-permission mappings.\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go` provides the same three-tier mapping for folders with `FolderViewActions`, `FolderEditActions`, and `FolderAdminActions`, which are also registered via `registerFolderRoles()`.\n\n**Permission Data Models:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go` defines the core data structures:\n- `RoleRegistration`: maps roles to their permissions and built-in role grants\n- `Permission`: represents an action-scope pair\n- `Role` and `RoleDTO`: represent stored roles and their metadata\nThe `BuiltInRolesWithParents()` function enforces role hierarchy where child roles inherit parent permissions.\n\n**Permission Guard Middleware:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go` implements the `Middleware()` function that wraps HTTP handlers with permission checks. It calls `authorize()` which evaluates permissions using an Evaluator against the user's permissions.\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/evaluator.go` defines permission evaluation:\n- `Evaluator` interface: represents permission requirements (action+scopes)\n- `EvalPermission()`: creates evaluators for specific actions\n- `EvalAll()` and `EvalAny()`: compose evaluators with AND/OR logic\n- `MutateScopes()`: resolves scope parameters (e.g., converting dashboard IDs to UIDs)\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/checker.go` implements `Checker()` which creates a closure that quickly checks if a user has specific scopes for an action using wildcard matching.\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/authorizer.go` provides `LegacyAccessClient` for authorization compatibility with k8s-style verbs, mapping them to RBAC actions.\n\n**Folder/Dashboard Permission Inheritance:**\n`pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go` implements permission inheritance:\n- `GetInheritedScopes()`: retrieves ancestor folder UIDs via `folderSvc.GetParents()`, returning their scopes\n- `NewFolderUIDScopeResolver()`: resolves folder scopes to include parent folder scopes\n- `NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver()`: resolves dashboard scopes to include both dashboard and parent folder scopes\n- `resolveDashboardScope()`: combines dashboard and folder scopes for permission evaluation\n\n`pkg/services/sqlstore/permissions/dashboard.go` implements the data layer filtering:\n- `NewAccessControlDashboardPermissionFilter()` creates filters with action sets mapping (e.g., \"folders:view\", \"folders:edit\", \"folders:admin\")\n- `accessControlDashboardPermissionFilter` applies SQL WHERE clauses that check both dashboard and inherited folder permissions\n\n**Low-Level Permission Types:**\n`pkg/services/dashboards/dashboardaccess/dashboard_access.go` defines the legacy three-tier `PermissionType` enum:\n- `PERMISSION_VIEW`: read access\n- `PERMISSION_EDIT`: write access\n- `PERMISSION_ADMIN`: admin access\nThis is converted to action-based permissions in the access control layer.\n\n**Main Interface:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go` defines:\n- `AccessControl` interface: evaluates permissions with `Evaluate()`\n- `Service` interface: manages roles and permissions with `DeclareFixedRoles()` and `GetUserPermissions()`\nThe service integrates all components to provide the complete permission enforcement system."
}

conversation_events.text:
# Grafana User Permission Model Role Coverage Audit

## Your Task

Find all Go source files in grafana/grafana that define and enforce the three-tier permission model (Viewer/Editor/Admin): the role-to-permission mapping, the permission guard middleware, and the folder/dashboard permission inheritance.

## Context

You are working on a codebase task involving repos from the compliance domain.

## Available Resources

The local `/workspace/` directory contains: sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec.

**Note:** Additional repositories are accessible via Sourcegraph MCP tools:
- `sg-evals/grafana--26d36ec` (grafana/grafana)
- `sg-evals/grafana-loki` (grafana/loki)
- `sg-evals/grafana-mimir` (grafana/mimir)

## Output Format

Create a file at `/workspace/answer.json` with your findings in the following structure:

```json
{
  "files": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go"}
  ],
  "symbols": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "SymbolName"}
  ],
  "chain": [
    {"repo": "org/repo-name", "path": "relative/path/to/file.go", "symbol": "FunctionName"}
  ],
  "text": "Narrative explanation of your findings, citing repos and file paths."
}
```

Include only the fields relevant to this task. Your answer is evaluated against a closed-world oracle — completeness matters.

## Evaluation

Your answer will be scored on:
- **File recall and precision**: Did you find all relevant files?
I'll help you find all Go source files that define and enforce the three-tier permission model in grafana/grafana. Let me start by exploring the repository structure.
Now let me create a comprehensive list of all the relevant files and compile them into the answer.json file:

Perfect! I've completed a comprehensive audit of the Grafana permission model implementation. Here's a summary of what I found:

## Summary

I've identified **13 core Go files** and **43 key symbols** that define and enforce Grafana's three-tier permission model (Viewer/Editor/Admin):

### Key Components Found:

**1. Role Definitions & Mapping (3 files)**
- `pkg/services/org/model.go` - Core role types (RoleViewer, RoleEditor, RoleAdmin)
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go` - Fixed role declarations with permission assignments
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go` - Role and Permission data structures

**2. Three-Tier Hierarchy (2 files)**
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go` - Maps DashboardViewActions/EditActions/AdminActions to the three roles
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go` - Maps FolderViewActions/EditActions/AdminActions to the three roles

**3. Permission Guard Middleware (4 files)**
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go` - HTTP middleware that enforces permission checks
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/evaluator.go` - Permission evaluation logic (EvalPermission, EvalAll, EvalAny)
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/checker.go` - Checker function for scope-based permission validation
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/authorizer.go` - Resource authorizer with legacy k8s verb support

**4. Folder/Dashboard Inheritance (2 files)**
- `pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go` - GetInheritedScopes() resolves parent folder permissions
- `pkg/services/sqlstore/permissions/dashboard.go` - SQL filters that apply inherited folder permissions

**5. Supporting Files (2 files)**
- `pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go` - Main AccessControl interface and Service
- `pkg/services/dashboards/dashboardaccess/dashboard_access.go` - Legacy PERMISSION_VIEW/EDIT/ADMIN constants

The answer has been saved to `/workspace/answer.json` with complete file paths, symbols, execution chains, and a detailed narrative explaining how these components work together.

  "text": "The Grafana permission model is built on a comprehensive three-tier role hierarchy (Viewer/Editor/Admin) defined across multiple packages:\n\n**Core Role Definitions:**\nThe three-tier role types are defined in `pkg/services/org/model.go` as `RoleViewer`, `RoleEditor`, and `RoleAdmin`. These are the base organization-level roles.\n\n**Role-to-Permission Mapping:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/roles.go` declares fixed roles and registers their assignments to the three built-in roles through the `DeclareFixedRoles()` function. The `BuildBasicRoleDefinitions()` function creates the basic role definitions with empty permissions initially. \n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/dashboard.go` explicitly maps dashboard permissions to the three tiers:\n- `DashboardViewActions` (read-only): assigned to \"Viewer\" role\n- `DashboardEditActions` (read+write+delete): assigned to \"Editor\" role  \n- `DashboardAdminActions` (read+write+delete+permissions): assigned to \"Admin\" role\nThe `registerDashboardRoles()` function registers these role-to-permission mappings.\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/ossaccesscontrol/folder.go` provides the same three-tier mapping for folders with `FolderViewActions`, `FolderEditActions`, and `FolderAdminActions`, which are also registered via `registerFolderRoles()`.\n\n**Permission Data Models:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/models.go` defines the core data structures:\n- `RoleRegistration`: maps roles to their permissions and built-in role grants\n- `Permission`: represents an action-scope pair\n- `Role` and `RoleDTO`: represent stored roles and their metadata\nThe `BuiltInRolesWithParents()` function enforces role hierarchy where child roles inherit parent permissions.\n\n**Permission Guard Middleware:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/middleware.go` implements the `Middleware()` function that wraps HTTP handlers with permission checks. It calls `authorize()` which evaluates permissions using an Evaluator against the user's permissions.\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/evaluator.go` defines permission evaluation:\n- `Evaluator` interface: represents permission requirements (action+scopes)\n- `EvalPermission()`: creates evaluators for specific actions\n- `EvalAll()` and `EvalAny()`: compose evaluators with AND/OR logic\n- `MutateScopes()`: resolves scope parameters (e.g., converting dashboard IDs to UIDs)\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/checker.go` implements `Checker()` which creates a closure that quickly checks if a user has specific scopes for an action using wildcard matching.\n\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/authorizer.go` provides `LegacyAccessClient` for authorization compatibility with k8s-style verbs, mapping them to RBAC actions.\n\n**Folder/Dashboard Permission Inheritance:**\n`pkg/services/dashboards/accesscontrol.go` implements permission inheritance:\n- `GetInheritedScopes()`: retrieves ancestor folder UIDs via `folderSvc.GetParents()`, returning their scopes\n- `NewFolderUIDScopeResolver()`: resolves folder scopes to include parent folder scopes\n- `NewDashboardUIDScopeResolver()`: resolves dashboard scopes to include both dashboard and parent folder scopes\n- `resolveDashboardScope()`: combines dashboard and folder scopes for permission evaluation\n\n`pkg/services/sqlstore/permissions/dashboard.go` implements the data layer filtering:\n- `NewAccessControlDashboardPermissionFilter()` creates filters with action sets mapping (e.g., \"folders:view\", \"folders:edit\", \"folders:admin\")\n- `accessControlDashboardPermissionFilter` applies SQL WHERE clauses that check both dashboard and inherited folder permissions\n\n**Low-Level Permission Types:**\n`pkg/services/dashboards/dashboardaccess/dashboard_access.go` defines the legacy three-tier `PermissionType` enum:\n- `PERMISSION_VIEW`: read access\n- `PERMISSION_EDIT`: write access\n- `PERMISSION_ADMIN`: admin access\nThis is converted to action-based permissions in the access control layer.\n\n**Main Interface:**\n`pkg/services/accesscontrol/accesscontrol.go` defines:\n- `AccessControl` interface: evaluates permissions with `Evaluate()`\n- `Service` interface: manages roles and permissions with `DeclareFixedRoles()` and `GetUserPermissions()`\nThe service integrates all components to provide the complete permission enforcement system."
}
